_comment: Nebula per-project Pi settings. See CLAUDE.md § AI Context Files.

subagents._comment: Per-project pi-subagents overrides. Three-tier Anthropic strategy with full provider diversification in fallback chains: each slot uses a different provider (anthropic primary → github-copilot → cursor-agent → openai-codex), no provider repeats within one chain so a single-provider outage burns at most one slot. Verified against the May 2026 model landscape (web search 26.05.2026). Cost rules: in github-copilot only models with multiplier ≤ 1x (haiku-4-5=0.33x, sonnet-4-5/4-6=1x, gpt-5.4=1x). EXCLUDED from copilot: opus-4-6 (5x), opus-4-7 (7.5x), gpt-5.5 (7.5x) — those go through openai-codex (flat-fee in ChatGPT subscription) instead. pi-cursor-agent v0.4.4 maps only sonnet-4-5/4-6 + opus-4-5/4-6/4-6-max (no haiku yet — cursor fallback for haiku tier substitutes sonnet-4-5). Thinking levels follow the Opus 4.7 native scale: medium=recon, high=workhorse, max=no token constraint for architecture / security review. Opus 4.7 only supports adaptive thinking; opus-4-6 supports [low,medium,high]; pi v0.72+ auto-clamps via thinkingLevelMap.

@vanillagreen/pi-hooks._comment: Nebula tuning: avoid duplicate work between lefthook.yml + .claude/hooks/ + this package. Workspace-wide clippy after every edit on 60+ crates costs 30-60s of UI-blocking even with sccache. Keep cheap-and-useful (bare-cd block) and one advisory pass at end of turn; let lefthook handle pre-commit per-crate clippy and let lsp_diagnostics (rust-analyzer) cover fast per-file feedback.

@vanillagreen/pi-output-policy._comment: In a large Rust workspace, cargo / clippy output can easily reach hundreds of KB per command. The balanced default mode is fine, but raise the inline tail a little so expanded clippy errors stay legible in chat without spilling to disk for every single failure.
